free_text: Yields and isomeric yield ratios of antimony isotopes
from the interaction of 159 MeV to 18.2 GeV protons
with uranium.
The target material was natural uranium in the form
of 47 mg/cm**2 or 94 mg/cm**2 metallic foils,or in the
form of U-O(3) powder,100-200 mg/cm**2 thickness. The
U-O(3) was produced from U-O(2)(NO(3)2)*6H(2)O.
The foil targets were mounted with guard foils and
monitor foils of aluminium, while the oxide powder was
irradiated in small tubes of aluminium foil.
A methane-flow proportional counter.
For 18.2-GeV irradiations.
Chemical yield, including the efficiency of the
isotope separator, was typically between 0.1 and 1.%.
Irradiation  time  was varied for different
products.
For 570- MeV beam.
For 18.- GeV beam
570 MeV
18 GeV
For 570 MeV
For 18. GeV
Tbls. 3-5 from JIN,29,2515
Half-lives were determined by authors.
Subentry O0577008 has been corrected
according to O.Schwerer's remarks.
Comments for half-lives are added.
Last checking has been done.
SD: SF4=SB-126-M1/G -> SB-126-G/M1 in
REACTION code in Subent 045. MONIT-ERR, EN-NRM were
added. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR, DECAY-DATA were added in
Subents 2-45. BIB updated in Subents 2-45. ERR-1 was
added in Subents 19-34, 36-41.
Isotopic yield relative to Sb-122. Sb-122 is shielded
nuclide.
Values from Nuclear Data Sheets
.The Uncertainty is reported by authors.
The errors given are standard deviations, taking into
account errors in counting efficiencies, half-lives,
counting statistics, contaminations and spread among
the parallel determinations relative to Sb-122.  The
errors in the monitoring cross-sections are not taken
into account, and the monitoring error of the Sb-122
relative to Na-24 is taken into account only for Sb-122
itself.
Half-life of Sb-115 has been measured by the
authors
.Number of determinations.
.The error of half-life take into account
standard deviation from the Least Squares Fitting for
each run and the Spread among different runs.
Systematic error may occur due to contamination from
the neighbouring isotopes. However, this contamination
was always below 4%, typically 1-1.5%, and could be
accurately corrected for.

authors
.Number of determinations.
.The Uncertainty is reported by authors.

.The error on half-life take into account
standard deviation from the Least Squares Fitting for
each run and the spread among different runs.
Systematic error may occur due to contamination from
the neighbouring isotopes. However, this contamination
was always below 4%, typically 1-1.5%, and could be
accurately corrected for.
In the case Sb-127 was counted in equilibrium with its
daughter, 9.3 hours Te-127. Due to a slightly premature
start of counting, the measured half-life of Sb-127 may
be long by as much as 1-2%.
